_publications._publicationTitle: Ex vivo to in vivo model of malignant peripheral nerve sheath tumors for precision oncology
_publications._fundingAgency: NTAP; NCI; Department of Defense CDMRP
_publications._context: PDX cells encapsulated in collagen-Matrigel microtissues within agarose microwell plates; viability assessed at 48 h as robust (>90%), good (>50%), or unusable (<50%); ex vivo drug response predicted in vivo response for MN-2, JH-2-002, JH-2-079-c, and WU-225

basicInfo.description: Patient-derived xenograft (PDX) cells encapsulated in collagen I/Matrigel microtissues fabricated by microfluidic droplet generation and cultured in agarose microwell plates; viability-based ex vivo drug sensitivity testing validated against matched in vivo PDX studies for NF1-associated MPNST
qualityControlMetrics: Viability assessment (Calcein AM / DRAQ5, Zeiss Axio Observer); compaction assay (projected area at 0, 2, and 5 days)
basicInfo.developerName: Angela C. Hirbe; David K. Wood; Christine A. Pratilas
basicInfo.developerAffiliation: Division of Oncology, Washington University School of Medicine; Department of Biomedical Engineering, University of Minnesota; Department of Oncology, Johns Hopkins University School of Medicine

modelLimitations: Requires freshly harvested PDX tumors processed through dissociation kits; viability success depends on PDX engraftment and tumor quality (some PDX classified as unusable); microtissue fabrication requires specialized microfluidic device; does not include stromal or immune components
